config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh mudah dikalahkan atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan dasar setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang mampu menyeimbangkan keduanya, bukan menempatkan individu sebagai korban demi kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that kind of framing makes me nervous because history shows how easily "the common good" can be used to excuse discrimination, censorship, or abuse of minorities. Society does have real needs, but those needs should be met by limiting rights only in narrow, justified cases, not by treating individual rights as disposable. To me, a healthy society protects people first and then asks them to contribute within those protections.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, kita memang sering diajarkan soal gotong royong dan kepentingan bersama, tetapi saya juga melihat betapa mudahnya dalih “demi masyarakat” dipakai untuk menekan suara minoritas atau membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan publik penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum agar mayoritas tidak berubah menjadi alat penindasan. Jadi, bagi saya, yang benar bukan mengorbankan individu demi kolektif, melainkan menyeimbangkan keduanya dengan hak individu sebagai pagar utama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights in any broad or default sense. From an American point of view, once we let “the public good” outrank rights too easily, it becomes a convenient excuse for censorship, discrimination, or government overreach, and history gives us plenty of reasons to be cautious. Society absolutely has legitimate needs, but those should be pursued through narrowly tailored limits, not by treating individual liberty as something optional. To me, a just society is one that starts with protecting the person and only restricts rights when there is a truly compelling reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari kacamata saya sebagai orang Indonesia, gotong royong itu penting, tetapi saya juga melihat bagaimana alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk membungkam minoritas, menekan kritik, atau melindungi penyalahgunaan kuasa. Karena itu, hak individu harus jadi pagar utama, sementara kepentingan masyarakat hanya boleh membatasi hak dalam keadaan yang benar-benar perlu dan jelas dasar hukumnya. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mengorbankan orang per orang, melainkan yang mampu menjaga keseimbangan tanpa menjadikan individu sebagai tumbal.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. In the United States, we have a long history of seeing how “the public good” can become a cover for censorship, discrimination, and government abuse, so I am very wary of giving the collective a default trump card. That said, I am not saying rights are absolute in every situation; public health, safety, and due process can justify limited restrictions, but only when they are narrow, necessary, and accountable. A good society protects individual rights first and then builds shared responsibility on top of that, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
